text.title: Guardrails Implementation Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for the implementation layer above guardrails-basics.

rule.statement: Configuration is a single GuardrailConfig dataclass; runtime reads from one source of truth. No scattered booleans.
rule.rationale: Scattered config drifts; single dataclass is auditable and testable.

rule.statement: Input checks ordered cheap-first (rule-based → moderation API → optional LLM). Output checks separated into validators (accept/block) and filters (transform).
rule.rationale: Mixing validators and filters produces unclear pipelines; ordering preserves the latency budget.

rule.statement: Validator vs filter is a hard distinction: validators return ok/blocked; filters return transformed-text. Never combine.
rule.rationale: Combined fns become untestable; separate concerns are observable.

rule.statement: Validator exceptions fail loud (raise + log + block per fail_closed). Never silently caught.
rule.rationale: Silent except hides incident severity; loud fail surfaces real problems early.

rule.statement: For high-throughput, async fan-out of independent checks via ThreadPoolExecutor. Track per-check latency separately.
rule.rationale: Sequential serial checks under high traffic exceed latency budget.

rule.statement: Every block emits a structured violation record (rule_id + severity + tenant + feature + input_hash). Retention ≥ 90 days.
rule.rationale: Without an audit trail postmortems cannot reconstruct the path; 90 days covers typical quarterly review windows.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → wire the pipeline per content/04-procedure.xml.
No production traffic OR sub-500ms latency budget → skip; use code-only checks.
